mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound compresses the equity discount rate continuously, providing persistent tailwind support to S&P 500 valuations. As long as FEDFUNDS remains below 1%, forward 20-day returns skew positive relative to unconditional baseline.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.09 at snapshot, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return during FEDFUNDS<1% periods is not significantly higher than the full-sample mean (t-test p>0.05), or Rank IC stays below 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate declines by more than 0.5 percentage points over a trailing three-month window from a cyclical peak, it signals an accelerating labor market recovery that raises expected corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 gains over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls more than 0.5pp over trailing 3 months from a local peak
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=7.4 at snapshot, elevated post-shock level
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of near-zero nominal rates (FEDFUNDS) and moderate-to-rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) implies a deeply negative real policy rate, which incentivizes capital rotation into equities as a real-return hedge, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month. The effect requires both conditions simultaneously; neither alone is sufficient.
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to outperform single-factor (FEDFUNDS-only) baseline by a statistically significant margin (p>0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery regime, a rebound in housing starts (HOUST) signals renewed consumer confidence and durable-goods demand, which feeds through to broader equity market strength over a quarterly horizon. The mechanism is regime-conditional: in late-cycle expansions the same HOUST rise instead signals overheating and has muted or reversed predictive power.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises for 2 consecutive months following a GDPC1 trough
failure_condition: During identified recovery-regime windows, HOUST upticks fail to precede positive 60-day forward returns in at least 55% of cases, or the direction reverses sign relative to late-cycle regime comparison.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects improving real economic output and corporate revenue momentum, translating into a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 prices over the subsequent week as markets incrementally price in the data.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO prints are not significantly different from returns following negative prints (t-test p>0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS rising) and declining unemployment (UNRATE falling) constitutes a confirmed labor-market inflection that reduces recession risk premia and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over one month. Neither series alone (e.g. payrolls rising while unemployment is flat due to labor force reentry) is sufficient to trigger the effect.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month increase and UNRATE month-over-month decrease simultaneously
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes show no significant outperformance versus PAYEMS-only trigger episodes (p>0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month surge in retail sales (RSAFS) above a threshold indicates unexpectedly strong consumer spending that triggers immediate short-term bullish repricing of consumer-linked equities and the broader index within a week.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes of RSAFS surges do not produce positive 5-day forward returns in at least 55% of instances, or effect is not statistically distinguishable from unconditional 5-day return distribution.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime with near-zero policy rates, a sustained acceleration in CPI (CPIAUCSL 12-month change rising above 2%) signals the early stage of reflation that boosts nominal corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 gains over a quarterly horizon; outside this regime, the same inflation signal instead raises rate-hike fears and depresses returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 12-month change exceeds 2% while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%
failure_condition: In the near-zero-rate regime, triggered reflation episodes fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 55% of instances, or the direction of the effect flips sign relative to normal-rate regimes without corresponding recovery-regime justification.
